summary: This cycle is clustering around major labs, coding agents, and open weights. The strongest signal is that people want better tool surfaces, tighter evals, and less fragile agent loops.

experimentQueue.title: Track lab releases against your workflow
experimentQueue.why: Major lab changes often shift the agent design space overnight.
experimentQueue.prompt: Map the newest lab release to a specific workflow in your stack and test whether it meaningfully reduces friction.

experimentQueue.title: Add durable memory to one workflow
experimentQueue.why: Persistent state is one of the main gaps between demos and useful agents.
experimentQueue.prompt: Give the agent a minimal memory layer, then replay the same task over multiple sessions and compare behavior.

experimentQueue.title: Measure local vs cloud model drift
experimentQueue.why: Open-weight releases keep changing the tradeoff surface for agents.
experimentQueue.prompt: Pick one agent task and compare a local open-weight model to a frontier model on latency, cost, and tool accuracy.

method: Hacker News Algolia search over agentic AI keywords
Reddit public search JSON across practical builder communities
Hugging Face model search for open-weight and lab-related drops
Best-effort RSS checks for major lab announcement feeds
